subject: [PATCH v3 03/10] nfsd: fix stale s2s_cp_stateids IDR entry for async COPY

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFS server (nfsd) where an IDR entry for async COPY operations was being pointed at temporary memory that is reused by subsequent requests, leading to potential crashes or data corruption when the IDR walker tries to access stale memory. The fix involves duplicating the fields first and then registering the stateid on the stable async_copy structure, ensuring that the IDR entry points to a valid and persistent location. This patch addresses an issue introduced in commit e0639dc5805a ('NFSD introduce async copy feature') and is part of a larger series of patches to fix various issues related to async COPY operations.
